clues.clue: Inspired by plant burrs, this hook-and-loop fastener became a household brand name.
A 3M chemist's weak adhesive accidentally created this repositionable sticky note.
Gideon Sundback perfected this interlocking-teeth fastener for clothing and bags.
Laszlo Biro lent his name in many countries to this smear-resistant writing instrument.
Percy Spencer invented this kitchen appliance after a candy bar melted near his radar set.
Reese's Cups are filled with chocolate and this creamy nut spread.
A foil-wrapped chocolate teardrop called a 'Kiss' is made by this brand.
Whoppers and Maltesers share this airy, toasted-grain center.
Each Ferrero Rocher hides a whole one of these round tree nuts in its center.
A Cadbury Creme Egg is filled with fondant dyed to mimic this golden part of a real egg.
Our home galaxy bears this milky-looking name.
This brilliant explosion marks the violent death of a massive star.
This collapsed object is so dense that not even light can escape it.
The bright 'North Star' also goes by this proper name.
The nearest star beyond the Sun is 'Proxima' followed by this constellation name.
An Alaska statute is often cited for banning waking this hibernating animal to take a photo.
An 1897 Indiana bill nearly legally redefined this circle-ratio constant to 3.2.
Carmel, California once required a permit to wear these tall shoes on its sloped streets.
Gainesville, Georgia 'requires' that this fried bird be eaten only with one's hands.
A reputed Kansas rule forbids topping cherry pie on Sundays with this cold dairy treat.
Samuel Morse lent his name to this dot-and-dash signaling system.
Gutenberg's 15th-century machine made mass-produced books possible.
Guglielmo Marconi is credited with the first practical wireless transmission of this signal.
Ray Tomlinson sent the first networked message of this '@'-addressed type in 1971.
Tim Berners-Lee proposed this hyperlinked information system in 1989.
clues.response: What is the World Wide Web?

final.clue: This brand's chocolate-and-peanut-butter cups regularly top surveys of favorite trick-or-treat candy.
